issue: Regional proliferation challenges including the the Democratic People's Republic of Korea, Iran and Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action

entries.position_summary: The United States insists Iran must never acquire a nuclear weapon and supports a negotiated resolution/new deal that addresses international concerns while enabling Iranian prosperity. It reports constructive talks toward such an agreement but notes more work remains.
entries.quote: As we have said con�nuously, Iran can never be allowed to obtain a nuclear weapon. The Trump administra�on believes that a nego�ated resolu�on – one that addresses the interna�onal community’s concerns while advancing Iranian prosperity – remains within reach. Talks toward a new deal with Iran have been construc�ve, and much more work remains to be done.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s accelerating nuclear and ballistic missile programs as unlawful and in violation of UN Security Council resolutions, and calls on NPT States Parties to join in denouncing this behavior.
entries.quote: • The DPRK is accelerating its unlawful nuclear weapons and ballistic missile programs in violation of multiple UN Security Council resolutions. We hope States Parties will join us in condemning its reckless and destabilizing behavior.

entries.position_summary: The country views Iran’s nuclear program as a prime challenge to the nonproliferation regime, asserting that Iran is producing highly enriched uranium without a credible civilian justification. It also accuses Iran of disregarding its NPT-mandated comprehensive safeguards obligations.
entries.quote: Despite the successes of the NPT in its 55 years in force, stressors con�nue to challenge the nonprolifera�on regime. Prime among these challenges con�nues to be Iran’s nuclear program Iran con�nues to accelerate its produc�on of highly enriched uranium – a material for which Iran has no credible civilian purpose. No other country in the world is enriching to this level that does not also possess nuclear weapons. Moreover, Iran has disregarded its obliga�ons under its NPT- mandated comprehensive safeguards agreement.

entries.position_summary: The United States opposes Iran developing or acquiring nuclear weapons and seeks to resolve the issue peacefully through diplomacy, implying support for negotiated solutions such as the JCPOA framework.
entries.quote: The president has made clear that the United States will not allow Iran to develop or acquire a nuclear weapon. It is our strong preference that this issue be resolved peacefully, through diplomatic means.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK for advancing its WMD and ballistic missile programs, characterizing them as destabilizing and in violation of multiple UN Security Council resolutions.
entries.quote: The DPRK is advancing its destabilizing weapons of mass destruction and ballistic missile programs in violation of multiple UN Security Council resolutions.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns the DPRK’s continued advancement of unlawful WMD and ballistic missile programs in violation of UN Security Council resolutions. It urges all NPT States Parties to fully implement these resolutions as a prerequisite for productive dialogue aimed at reversing the DPRK’s programs.
entries.quote: We note that the DPRK also continues to advance unlawful WMD and ballistic missile programs through sanctions evasion, violating multiple UN Security Council resolutions. We call upon all states parties to fully implement these resolutions, which is necessary for dialogue to play a role in reversing the DPRK’s WMD and missile programs.

entries.position_summary: The United States warns that Iran is escalating its nuclear program by producing highly enriched uranium and failing to fully cooperate with the IAEA. It argues that Iran is not meeting its safeguards obligations, preventing the Agency from assuring the program is exclusively peaceful.
entries.quote: The United States is profoundly concerned by Tehran’s sustained path of nuclear escalation. Iran is the only country without nuclear weapons that is producing highly enriched uranium. For five years, Iran has refused to fully cooperate with the IAEA and abide by its safeguards commitments; due to this intransigence, the Agency remains unable to provide critical assurances that the program is exclusively peaceful.

entries.position_summary: The statement condemns Iran for accelerating production and accumulation of highly enriched uranium without a credible civilian purpose, noting this level of enrichment is atypical for non-nuclear-weapon states. It further alleges Iran is disregarding its NPT-mandated comprehensive safeguards obligations.
entries.quote: Iran has accelerated its production and accumulation of highly enriched uranium – a material for which Iran has no credible civilian purpose. No other country in the world is enriching to this level that does not also possess nuclear weapons. Moreover, Iran has disregarded its obligations under its NPT-mandated comprehensive safeguards agreement.

entries.position_summary: The statement criticizes Iran for failing to cooperate with the IAEA on undeclared nuclear material, not implementing its Additional Protocol and modified Code 3.1, and expanding enrichment to 60% with advanced centrifuges. It argues these actions lack a credible civilian purpose and urges collective pressure for Iran to comply with its safeguards obligations.
entries.quote: Equally concerning are Iran’s failures to provide the IAEA with technically credible clarifications regarding undeclared locations where the IAEA detected the presence of nuclear material. Iran is not implementing its Additional Protocol and modified Code 3.1 of its safeguards subsidiary arrangements, both of which remain in force. Iran is also expanding its uranium enrichment infrastructure, deploying advanced centrifuges and producing HEU up to 60%, none of which have a credible civilian purpose. We must all insist that Iran

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s ongoing unlawful nuclear and ballistic missile programs, viewing them as a threat to international peace and security and to the global nonproliferation regime.
entries.quote: The DPRK continues its unlawful nuclear and ballistic missile programs, threatening international peace and security and the global nonproliferation regime.

entries.position_summary: The statement asserts that Iran has not fully addressed IAEA inquiries regarding indications of possible undeclared nuclear material and activities, signaling concern over safeguards compliance. It implicitly urges Iran to cooperate fully with the IAEA to resolve these issues.
entries.quote: And Iran has not yet fully answered questions from the IAEA about indications of possible undeclared nuclear material and activities on its territory.

entries.position_summary: The United States condemns the DPRK’s unlawful nuclear and missile programs and its threatening rhetoric, viewing them as a serious threat to international peace and the nonproliferation regime. It seeks complete, verifiable denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula through sustained diplomacy and insists on the DPRK’s return to the NPT and IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: Chair, The DPRK’s unlawful and continuing nuclear and ballistic missile programs constitute a serious and direct threat to international peace and security and to the global nonproliferation regime. The DPRK’s threatening and irresponsible rhetoric has also intensified, including by characterizing some of its missile launches and other military activities as “trial runs” for the use of tactical nuclear weapons. Our goal remains the complete and verifiable denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula. In that regard, the United States is committed to serious, calibrated, and sustained diplomacy with the DPRK, and we will continue to insist on its return to the NPT and IAEA safeguards.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports CTBT entry into force, backs its verification regime, funds and supplies IMS data, and continues its zero‑yield testing moratorium while urging China and Russia to adhere to similar moratoria and the DPRK to declare and maintain one, with greater transparency from all. It hosts observers at the Nevada National Security Site and commits to transparency in stockpile stewardship. The United States also supports negotiating an FMCT at the Conference on Disarmament in line with CD/1299 as a key next step toward disarmament.
entries.quote: 2000 Steps One and Two; 2010 Actions Ten through Fourteen: CTBT and Test Moratorium: On Steps One and Two, the United States remains committed to the CTBT’s entry into force and strongly supports the treaty’s verification regime. The United States is the largest financial contributor to the CTBTO PrepCom, provides significant voluntary contributions, and maintains high data availability from the IMS stations the United States is responsible for. The United States has not conducted a nuclear explosive test since 1992 and continues to observe its zero- yield moratorium on nuclear explosive testing. We urge the PRC and Russia to adhere to their respective zero-yield moratoria on nuclear explosive testing and the DPRK to declare and maintain such moratorium. We also urge the PRC, Russia, and the DPRK to provide transparency into their testing programs. For its part, the United States has continued hosting international observers at the Nevada National Security Site (NNSS), and we are committed to transparency in our stockpile stewardship and nonproliferation activities at NNSS and other nuclear security enterprise sites. 2000 Steps Three and Four; 2010 Actions Six and Fifteen: Progress on FMCT and Other Efforts at the CD: On Steps Three and Four, we support negotiation of an FMCT at the Conference on Disarmament as the essential next multilateral step toward achieving the ultimate goal of a world without nuclear weapons. Negotiations, which should take place in accordance with CD/1299, are long overdue.

entries.position_summary: The country expresses concern over Iranian officials' statements implying a capability to acquire nuclear weapons and calls for rejecting such rhetoric. It urges collective condemnation to deter proliferation-related threats.
entries.quote: Concerning Iran, we note the recent statements by Iranian officials signaling a capability to acquire nuclear weapons should it decide to do so. We must all join in rejecting such language.

entries.position_summary: The United States asserts that Iran is failing to meet its IAEA safeguards obligations, including by not cooperating on outstanding issues related to potential undeclared nuclear material and activities. It criticizes Iran’s termination of modified Code 3.1 and suspension of the Additional Protocol, arguing these steps undermine transparency and prevent the IAEA from assuring the peaceful nature of Iran’s program.
entries.quote: The United States remains deeply concerned that, to date, Iran has failed to provide the cooperation required by its safeguards obligations under the Treaty. The burden is on Iran to resolve outstanding safeguards issues. It should do this by providing credible information necessary to clarify IAEA questions regarding potential undeclared nuclear material and activities. Iran’s unilateral decision to terminate implementation of modified Code 3.1 is contrary to its legal obligations, reducing international confidence that all nuclear facilities under construction in Iran are declared. Iran’s decision to end implementation of its Additional Protocol is also deeply troubling. Because of Iran’s unsatisfactory cooperation, the IAEA remains unable to provide assurances regarding the

entries.position_summary: The country argues that Iran’s production of 60% HEU and use of advanced centrifuges lack a credible civilian purpose, heighten proliferation risks, and should be replaced by confidence-building steps, including resolving IAEA safeguards issues. It states it remains committed to diplomacy and a return to full JCPOA implementation, asserting that Iran blocked a swift return by demanding closure of IAEA investigations.
entries.quote: Further, Iran’s production of highly enriched uranium and deployment of advanced centrifuges amplify these legitimate concerns. Iran’s production of HEU up to 60% has no credible civilian purpose. If Iran’s purpose was genuinely peaceful, we should expect it to take actions that build international confidence and de-escalate tensions, rather than engage in nuclear provocations that pose grave proliferation risks. I would note that the United States remains committed to diplomacy. It was Iran that rejected a swift return to full implementation of the JCPOA almost a year ago, demanding the closure of the IAEA’s open safeguards investigations as part of the agreement. As we’ve made clear, the power to resolve these issues is in Iran’s hands alone.

entries.position_summary: The delegation argues that while they do not seek to amend the NPT or restrict the legitimate right to withdraw, any withdrawal undermines the security benefits for remaining Parties. They advocate holding any withdrawing state fully accountable for violations committed prior to withdrawal, citing the DPRK case and the lack of RevCon consensus to date.
entries.quote: Chair, Since the DPRK announced its withdrawal from the NPT in 2003, four Review Conferences have been unable to reach consensus on ways to discourage similar action by another State Party in the future, though the 2022 RevCon came close. We are not proposing to amend the Treaty or to limit the legitimate right of withdrawal. However, any withdrawal from the NPT can diminish the security benefits the remaining NPT Parties derive from the Treaty. Holding a withdrawing state fully accountable for any violations of the Treaty committed prior to its withdrawal does not infringe upon that state’s right of withdrawal but rather

entries.position_summary: The delegation condemns Syria’s safeguards non-compliance, the DPRK’s unchecked nuclear and ballistic advances, and Russia’s seizure of Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia NPP, urging accountability for actions undermining the nonproliferation regime. It criticizes Russia and the PRC for expanding and diversifying their nuclear arsenals while showing little interest in arms control, and warns about destabilizing coordination among Russia, the PRC, the DPRK, and Iran.
entries.quote: agreement, Syria’s non-compliance with its comprehensive safeguards agreement, the DPRK’s unchecked advances of its nuclear and ballistic weapons programs, and Russia’s seizure of Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Plant. Furthermore, Russia and the PRC are expanding and diversifying their nuclear weapons arsenals — showing little or no interest in arms control. Russia, the PRC, and the DPRK, together with Iran, are increasingly cooperating and coordinating with each other in ways that run counter to peace and stability and exacerbate regional tensions. We will have more to say on these topics in our statement on regional issues; however, my delegation wishes to stress that part of keeping the nonproliferation regime strong is holding those whose actions contradict it accountable.

entries.position_summary: The country emphasizes a preference for diplomacy but asserts that Iran is defying its NPT commitments, urging all NPT States Parties to be concerned. It signals that Iran’s behavior poses a serious regional proliferation challenge.
entries.quote: At the same �me, our preference for diplomacy does not alter the fact that Iran con�nues to defy its NPT commitments. That should concern all states that are party to this treaty.

entries.position_summary: The statement supports a peaceful, negotiated resolution to address concerns about Iran’s nuclear program, indicating openness to diplomacy that advances security and prosperity for both the U.S. and Iran.
entries.quote: President Trump has also made clear that he continues to believe that a negotiated resolution – one that peacefully addresses the threats posed by Iran’s nuclear program and advances the prosperity and security of both our nations – remains possible.

entries.position_summary: The country supports holding states accountable for fulfilling IAEA safeguards obligations under Article III. It also aligns with the joint statement on the DPRK, emphasizing that the DPRK’s nuclear program remains an active concern within the NPT.
entries.quote: We also welcomed the chair’s first draft recommendation to hold states accountable for their safeguards obligations. This is an essential element of Article III of the NPT and a measure of the overall health of the nonproliferation regime. And we note that 82 States Parties – including the United States – signed onto France and the Republic of Korea’s joint statement on the DPRK’s nuclear program, demonstrating that the DPRK’s destabilizing actions are not a closed issue in the NPT.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns Russia for enabling the DPRK’s weapons capabilities, unlawfully procuring DPRK ballistic missiles and munitions, and shielding Pyongyang from UN Security Council action, labeling the DPRK an NPT violator. It asserts Russia is doing this to sustain its war against Ukraine.
entries.quote: Unfortunately, rather than enforce UN Security Council resolutions, the Russian Federation chooses to enable the weapons capabilities of the DPRK — an NPT violator. The Russian Federation is unlawfully procuring DPRK ballistic missiles and munitions, and shields the DPRK from condemnation or UN Security Council action. And let’s be clear, Russia is doing this to sustain its illegal war against Ukraine.

entries.position_summary: The statement highlights Iran’s failure to fully cooperate with IAEA safeguards as a key challenge to the NPT, signaling concern over Iran’s compliance. It underscores that such non-cooperation threatens international security and stability and implies a need for Iran to meet its safeguards obligations.
entries.quote: Notwithstanding its many contributions, the NPT continues to face challenges that, if left unaddressed, will pose an increasing threat to international security and stability. These challenges include Iran’s failure to fully cooperate with the IAEA as required by its safeguards

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes Iran for expanding its nuclear program without credible civilian justification, failing to cooperate with the IAEA to clarify its activities, and making provocative statements about nuclear weapons capability.
entries.quote: • Iran is expanding its nuclear program in ways that have no credible civilian justification, failing to provide the IAEA with the necessary cooperation to prove otherwise, and engaging in loose talk about nuclear weapons capability.

entries.position_summary: The United States views the DPRK’s missile and WMD programs as significant security threats and calls for the complete denuclearization of North Korea. It urges the DPRK to return to the NPT and to compliance with IAEA safeguards.
entries.quote: Similarly, the DPRK’s ballis�c missile and weapons of mass destruc�on programs pose significant security threats. The United States calls for the complete denucleariza�on of North Korea and its return to the NPT and IAEA

entries.position_summary: The country supports complete denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula and is ready for practical dialogue to secure the DPRK’s return to the NPT and full compliance with IAEA safeguards. It endorses France’s joint statement highlighting the DPRK’s threat to the global nonproliferation regime and urges others to join. This implies support for NPT universality and opposition to NPT withdrawal.
entries.quote: Our goal remains the complete denuclearization of the Korean Peninsula, and we stand ready to pursue calibrated, practical dialogue that results in the DPRK’s return to, and full compliance with, the NPT and IAEA safeguards. And in this regard, we fully endorse France’s joint statement addressing the DPRK’s threat to the global nuclear nonproliferation regime and urge all parties to do the same.

entries.position_summary: The statement criticizes China’s rapid, opaque nuclear buildup and rejects China’s proposed No First Use treaty as non-credible and unverifiable. It also condemns Russia’s coercive nuclear rhetoric and highlights concern over Russia’s increased military cooperation with the DPRK.
entries.quote: Such falsehoods distract from the truly worrisome issues at hand, including China’s rapid and opaque nuclear weapons build-up, and Russia’s coercive nuclear rhetoric and increased military cooperation with the DPRK. We were dismayed by some States Parties’ support for China’s proposal for a non- credible, unverifiable No First Use Treaty. We must ask – what good is a statement from China that it will not use nuclear weapons first, when its nuclear build-up appears to contradict to its stated policy?

entries.position_summary: The country underscores the IAEA’s critical role, supporting its verification activities in Iran and its efforts to maintain nuclear safety and security in Ukraine.
entries.quote: The IAEA is playing a critical role to address immediate challenges, whether by providing for verification in Iran or maintaining safety and security in Ukraine.

entries.position_summary: The United States supports engaging on regional proliferation challenges and endorses the goal of NPT universality. It reaffirms full commitment to strengthening and upholding the Treaty.
entries.quote: The United States continues to welcome the opportunity to discuss regional proliferation challenges that jeopardize our collective security. As such, the United States will continue to support the long-term goal of the NPT, including its universality, and we remain fully committed to efforts to strengthen and uphold the Treaty.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s continued provocative ballistic missile testing, including numerous launches and multiple ICBM tests, characterizing this as egregious ongoing behavior.
entries.quote: Meanwhile, Chair, the DPRK carries on with flagrantly egregious behavior. The DPRK continues to conduct provocative ballistic missile tests, including 100 such tests and 13 of intercontinental range since

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s ongoing WMD and ballistic missile programs as violations of UN Security Council resolutions and a threat to regional and global security. It expresses strong concern over the DPRK’s continued nuclear activities and the possibility of a seventh nuclear test, as well as its bellicose rhetoric.
entries.quote: Meanwhile, Chair, the DPRK continues to advance its destabilizing weapons of mass destruction and ballistic missile programs in violation of multiple UN Security Council resolutions. These programs pose a threat to all of us and undermine security in the region. We note with significant concern the DPRK’s ongoing nuclear activities, including the possibility of a seventh nuclear test, and its continued irresponsible and bellicose rhetoric.

entries.position_summary: The country condemns the DPRK’s escalatory rhetoric and missile activities, noting Pyongyang’s portrayal of launches as trial runs for tactical nuclear weapon use. It also criticizes the DPRK’s malicious cyber activities used to finance and advance its unlawful weapons programs.
entries.quote: 2022. Pyongyang’s rhetoric has become increasingly escalatory and aggressive, going so far as to characterize certain of its missile launches and other military activities as trial runs for the use of tactical nuclear weapons. And it continues to generate revenue through malicious cyber activities that target all countries to further advance its unlawful weapons programs.

entries.position_summary: The country criticizes Iran for obstructing NPT proceedings by blocking a Working Group and preventing the PrepCom Chair’s factual summary. It urges that such actions not be repeated at the second Preparatory Committee, emphasizing the need for a functional, rules-based review process.
entries.quote: Further, Iran’s pattern of obstructionist behavior in multilateral processes undermines the spirit of diplomacy and compromise. We all recall Iran joined Russia in blocking the Working Group last year, and, in a significant abuse of procedure, blocked the PrepCom Chair from providing a factual summary of events. We must insist this not be repeated at this second Preparatory Committee meeting.

entries.position_summary: The United States commits to the complete denuclearization of the DPRK in close coordination with allies, particularly the ROK and Japan. It supports full implementation of UN Security Council resolutions and enhanced monitoring to counter sanctions evasion that enables North Korea’s nuclear and missile programs.
entries.quote: The United States will pursue the complete denuclearization of North Korea, consulting closely with the Republic of Korea, Japan, and other allies and partners to achieve this goal and deter DPRK aggression. We will continue to cooperate with our allies and partners to fully implement UN Security Council resolutions and monitor the sanctions evasion which enables the DPRK’s unlawful nuclear and ballistic missile programs and destabilizing behavior.

entries.position_summary: The delegation asserts that Syria’s construction of an undeclared plutonium production reactor at Dair Alzour constitutes non-compliance with NPT Article III and its required IAEA safeguards agreement. They reiterate this finding to underscore the need for accountability under the safeguards regime.
entries.quote: Chair, we again reiterate that Syria’s construction of an undeclared plutonium production reactor at Dair Alzour constitutes non-compliance with NPT Article III and its NPT-required safeguards agreement. The only SENSITIVE BUT UNCLASSIFIED

entries.position_summary: The country asserts that Iran’s nuclear program is peaceful and that all Iranian nuclear material is under IAEA safeguards in line with NPT Article III. It implies recognition of Iran’s compliance within the Iran-related regional proliferation context.
entries.quote: peaceful nature of Iran’s nuclear program, or that Iran has placed all nuclear material under safeguards as required by NPT Article III.

entries.position_summary: The United States underscores ongoing regional proliferation concerns, asserting that Iran, the DPRK, and Syria continue to threaten international peace and security and should act responsibly. It situates these concerns within NPT discussions on the Middle East, including implementation of the 1995 Resolution on the Middle East.
entries.quote: Statement by the United States Cluster 2 – Specific Issue Regional Issues, including with respect to the Middle East and the Implementation of the 1995 Resolution on the Middle East Second Preparatory Committee for the 2026 Conference of the Parties to the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons Full Version by Ambassador Adam Scheinman, Special Representative of the President for Nuclear Nonproliferation Geneva, Switzerland July 29, 2024 Chair, As we reported last year, the three parties we persistently call upon to act responsibly – Iran, the DPRK, and Syria – continue to threaten international peace and security and that of their own citizens.

entries.position_summary: The country reaffirms commitment to a world without nuclear weapons while warning that Russia, China, and North Korea are rapidly expanding and diversifying their arsenals. It calls for collective pressure on these states to undertake immediate nuclear risk-reduction measures.
entries.quote: Such a step would not reflect an abandonment of our principles or commitment to pursuing the shared goal of a world without nuclear weapons. But we cannot ignore the current security environment in which Russia, the PRC, and the DPRK are all expanding and diversifying their arsenals at breakneck pace. That is why we need your collective voices in demanding that all these countries engage in nuclear risk reduction efforts without further delay.
